description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular, rounded wooden cabinet featuring a rich, reddish-brown finish and visible wear. The front panel displays a large, oval-shaped glass frequency indicator with the brand 'Zenith' and a set of analog dials and push buttons below for tuning and volume control. Three horizontal grille slats are present on the left side of the front, likely covering the speaker area. The back of the radio is open, revealing internal components such as vacuum tubes, a speaker, and wiring, with a long, thin power or antenna cord extending outward. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, showing the wood grain and some scuff marks. This radio receiver is a classic example of mid-20th-century home electronics, designed to sit on a table or shelf in an urban environment.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a glass frequency display, analog controls, and visible internal components at the back. The object features a reddish-brown finish, rounded edges, and a classic mid-century design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the glass frequency indicator, analog dials, push buttons, and speaker grille slats, with the brand 'Zenith' visible.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the smooth, rounded wooden surface with no controls or openings, and the cord extending from the back.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the open back with internal components such as vacuum tubes, a speaker, and wiring, as well as the attached cord.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the rounded wooden surface and the horizontal grille slats wrapping around the side, with the cord trailing from the back.
